sections.section: A. Introduction
sections.summary: Introductory heading 'A. Introduction' only; no substantive content provided.

sections.section: B. Terrestrial Code content
sections.summary: Explains Terrestrial Code structure and usage: defines that key cross-chapter terms are in the Glossary (italicised and hyperlinked online), that '(under study)' denotes provisions not adopted by the World Assembly of Delegates, and summarizes the purpose of Sections 1–7 (diagnosis/notification, import risk analysis, Veterinary Services standards, prevention/control, trade sanitary measures, production-level preventive measures and animal welfare) and that Sections 8–16 set commodity-specific standards to prevent listed pathogenic agents taking into account exporting status and applicable risk-reduction measures.

sections.section: ## General provisions
sections.summary: States the chapter's aim to mitigate spread and human health risk from Brucella abortus, B. melitensis and B. suis (excluding vaccine strains), defines 'Brucella' and enumerates animal categories covered (bovids, sheep and goats, pigs, camelids, cervids and European hare), defines a 'case' as an animal infected with Brucella, and (in a separate provision) defines infection as either isolation of Brucella from an animal sample or a positive diagnostic test with an epidemiological link and directs Veterinary Authorities to require the chapter's Brucella-related import/transit conditions (except for commodities in Article 8.4.2) while referencing the Terrestrial Manual for diagnostic test and vaccine standards.

sections.section:
## Article 8.4.2.

## Safe commodities
sections.summary: Article 8.4.2 lists commodities for which Veterinary Authorities should not require any Brucella-related conditions regardless of exporting-country Brucella status: skeletal muscle meat, brain and spinal cord, digestive tract, thymus, thyroid and parathyroid glands and derived products, cured hides and skins, and gelatine, collagen, tallow and protein meal.

sections.section: ## C H A P T E R   8 . 4 .
sections.summary: Chapter heading 'CHAPTER 8.4.' indicating the subject 'Infection with Brucella abortus, B. melitensis and B. suis' without substantive provisions in the provided text.

sections.section: ## I N F E C T I O N   W I T H B R U C E L L A   A B O R T U S , B . M E L I T E N S I S A N D B .   S U I S
sections.summary: Section title 'INFECTION WITH BRUCELLA ABORTUS, B. MELITENSIS AND B. SUIS' presented as a heading; no substantive text provided.

sections.section: General conditions and methods for surveillance
sections.summary: Heading 'General conditions and methods for surveillance' only; the provided text contains no detailed surveillance provisions.

sections.section: Surveillance strategies
sections.summary: Heading 'Surveillance strategies' only; no substantive content provided in the excerpt.

sections.section: ## General provisions
sections.summary: States that the pig (Sus scrofa, domestic and wild) is the only natural host for classical swine fever virus (CSFV) and notes that the chapter makes a distinction between categories relevant to the disease.

sections.section: ## Safe commodities
sections.summary: Specifies safe commodities for CSF trade for which Veterinary Authorities should not require CSF-related conditions regardless of exporting-country or zone status: meat in a hermetically sealed container with an F0 value of 3 or above, and gelatine.

sections.section: ## C H A P T E R   1 5 . 2 .
sections.summary: Chapter heading 'CHAPTER 15.2.' for 'Infection with classical swine fever virus' with no substantive provisions in the provided text.

sections.section: ## I N F E C T I O N   W I T H   C L A S S I C A L   S W I N E   F E V E R   V I R U S
sections.summary: Section title 'INFECTION WITH CLASSICAL SWINE FEVER VIRUS' presented as a heading; no substantive provisions included in the excerpt.

sections.section: ## Article 15.2.1.
sections.summary: Heading only (Article 15.2.1); no substantive provision in this section.

sections.section: Country or zone free from CSF
sections.summary: Heading only for 'Country or zone free from CSF'; no substantive provisions provided in this section.

sections.section: Compartment free from CSF
sections.summary: A compartment free from CSF must be established and granted bilateral recognition in accordance with this chapter's requirements and the principles set out in Chapters 4.4 and 4.5.

sections.section: Country or zone infected with CSFV
sections.summary: A country or zone is considered infected with CSFV when it does not fulfil the requirements for acceptance as a CSF‑free country or zone.

sections.section: Establishment of a containment zone within a country or zone previously free from CSF
sections.summary: When CSF outbreaks occur in a previously free country/zone (including protection zones), a containment zone comprising all epidemiologically linked outbreaks may be established per Article 4.4.7 to limit spread, and the Veterinary Authority must submit documented evidence promptly to WOAH while surveillance must address wild/feral pigs and prevent their dispersion; the free status of areas outside the containment zone is suspended during establishment but may be reinstated once WOAH approves the containment zone.

sections.section:
Article 15.2.7.

## Recovery of free status
sections.summary: A previously free country/zone may recover CSF‑free status after surveillance per Article 15.2.32 yields negative results and three months have passed after: (1) disinfection of the last affected establishment following stamping‑out without vaccination; (2) disinfection plus slaughter of all vaccinated animals after emergency vaccination with slaughter; or (3) disinfection after emergency vaccination without slaughter when validated methods (Terrestrial Manual Ch.3.8.3) can distinguish vaccinated from infected pigs.

sections.section:
Article 15.2.8.

## Direct transfer of pigs within a country from an infected zone to a free zone for slaughter
sections.summary: Pigs may be transported from an infected zone to a free zone only directly by mechanised vehicle to the nearest designated slaughterhouse under strict conditions (no introductions or clinical signs for 30 days, at least three months under approved biosecurity at origin, no CSF within 10 km for three months, vehicle disinfected before loading and biosecure transport without contact with other pigs, and slaughterhouse under approved biosecurity and restricted for export until used and disinfected); the animals must have favourable ante‑ and post‑mortem inspections (Ch.6.2) and the meat treated per Article 15.2.23 and kept separate until treated.

sections.section: Article 15.2.9. Recommendations for importation from countries, zones or compartments free from CSF
sections.summary: Heading only (Article 15.2.9) for recommendations on importation from CSF‑free countries/zones/compartments for domestic and captive wild pigs; no substantive attestations in this section text.

sections.section: Article 15.2.10. Recommendations for importation from countries or zones infected with CSFV
sections.summary: For imports of domestic and captive wild pigs from CSFV‑infected countries/zones, Veterinary Authorities should require an international veterinary certificate attesting the pigs showed no clinical signs on the day of shipment and either were kept since birth or for three months in a CSF‑free compartment or were isolated 28 days in quarantine with negative virological and serological tests (sample ≥21 days after entry), and that pigs are not vaccinated nor progeny of vaccinated sows unless validated DIVA methods exist (Terrestrial Manual Ch.3.8.3).

sections.section: Article 15.2.11. Recommendations for importation from countries, zones or compartments free from CSF
sections.summary: Heading/intro only (Article 15.2.11) introducing recommendations for importation of semen of domestic and captive wild pigs and stating a certificate should be required, but no specific attestations are listed in this section.

sections.section: For semen of domestic and captive wild pigs
sections.summary: Veterinary Authorities should require a certificate for semen showing donor males were kept since birth or for at least three months prior to collection in a CSF‑free country/zone/compartment, showed no clinical signs on the day of collection, and that semen was collected, processed and stored in accordance with Chapters 4.6 and 4.7.

sections.section:
Article 15.2.12.

## Recommendations for importation from countries or zones infected with CSFV
sections.summary: Heading only (Article 15.2.12) introducing recommendations for importation of semen from CSFV‑infected countries/zones; no substantive attestations in this section text.

sections.section: Recommendations for importation from countries, zones or compartments free from CSF
sections.summary: Heading only for recommendations concerning in vivo derived embryos of domestic pigs; no substantive provisions provided in this section.

sections.section: For fresh meat of domestic and captive wild pigs
sections.summary: Veterinary Authorities should require a certificate that the entire consignment of fresh meat comes from pigs kept in a CSF‑free country/zone/compartment or imported per Articles 15.2.9/15.2.10 and that they were slaughtered in an approved slaughterhouse subject to favourable ante‑ and post‑mortem inspections (Ch.6.2).

sections.section: Recommendations  for  importation  from  countries  or  zones  infected  with  CSFV,  where  an  official  control programme exists
sections.summary: For fresh meat from CSFV‑infected countries/zones with an official control programme, Veterinary Authorities should require a certificate confirming the source pigs complied with Article 15.2.10, were transported under Veterinary Authority supervision in disinfected vehicles directly to an approved export slaughterhouse without contact with other pigs, that no CSF was detected in that slaughterhouse since its last disinfection before slaughter, that ante‑ and post‑mortem inspections were favourable (Ch.6.2), and that appropriate post‑slaughter precautions prevented cross‑contamination.

sections.section: For fresh meat of domestic pigs and captive wild pigs
sections.summary: Veterinary Authorities should require a certificate for fresh meat confirming pigs met Article 15.2.10, were transported under official supervision in disinfected vehicles directly to an approved slaughterhouse without contact with other non‑compliant pigs, were slaughtered in an export‑approved facility with no CSF detected since its last disinfection, underwent favourable ante‑ and post‑mortem inspections (Ch.6.2), and that post‑slaughter measures prevented cross‑contamination.

sections.section: Recommendations for the importation of meat products of pigs
sections.summary: Importing Veterinary Authorities should require certificates attesting meat products were either prepared exclusively from fresh meat meeting Articles 15.2.15/15.2.16 in a facility approved for export (processing only meat meeting those conditions) or processed per Article 15.2.23 in an approved facility with precautions against cross‑contamination; the section also sets analogous certificate/processing requirements for bristles, litter/manure, skins/trophies and other pig commodities, and prescribes swill inactivation procedures (either ≥90°C for ≥60 minutes with stirring or saturated steam at ≥121°C for ≥10 minutes at 2 bar).

sections.section: ## Recommendations for the importation of bristles
sections.summary: Veterinary Authorities should require a certificate that bristles originated from pigs in a CSF‑free country/zone/compartment and were processed in an export‑approved facility, or that they were processed per Article 15.2.25 in an approved facility with precautions to avoid post‑processing cross‑contamination.

sections.section: ## Recommendations for the importation of litter and manure from pigs
sections.summary: Veterinary Authorities should require a certificate that litter or manure originated from pigs in a CSF‑free country/zone/compartment and were processed in an export‑approved facility, or were processed per Article 15.2.26 in an approved facility with measures to prevent cross‑contamination after processing.

sections.section: ## Recommendations for the importation of skins and trophies from pigs
sections.summary: Veterinary Authorities should require a certificate that skins or trophies originated from pigs in a CSF‑free country/zone/compartment and were processed in an export‑approved facility, or were processed per Article 15.2.27 in an approved facility with precautions to prevent post‑processing cross‑contamination.

sections.section: ## Recommendations for the importation of other pig commodities
sections.summary: Veterinary Authorities of importing countries should require an international veterinary certificate confirming that pig commodities either (1) originated from domestic or captive wild pigs in a country/zone/compartment free from CSF and were processed in a facility approved by the Veterinary Authority for export, or (2) were processed in a manner demonstrated to inactivate CSFV in an approved facility and thereafter handled with appropriate precautions to avoid cross‑contamination with CSFV.

sections.section: ## Procedures for the inactivation of CSFV in swill
sections.summary: To inactivate CSFV in swill use one of: (a) maintain swill at ≥90°C for ≥60 minutes with continuous stirring; or (b) maintain under saturated steam at ≥121°C for ≥10 minutes at an absolute pressure of 2 bar.
